behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/wiring error between manifest validation and plugin execution gate. Required setup: real loader wiring over a temp plugin directory with an invalid manifest plus an entry module that would produce an observable side effect if imported. Unit is too low because the key failure is 'rejected before any code ... executed' (validation must block import). Higher levels rejected: no real network/OS primitive/subprocess requirement beyond local filesystem, no AI dependency, no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin folder with `manifest.json` missing a required field and an entry file that writes a sentinel on import. Run loader discovery+validation+init. Assert loader reports manifest rejection and sentinel side effect never occurs.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's `onLoad` hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: dependency-resolution/order orchestration error across manifest parser, graph resolver, and hook dispatcher. Required setup: two real plugin packages wired through the loader, each logging `onLoad` call order. Unit is too low because cross-module ordering is the behavior under test. Higher levels rejected: behavior text is local plugin dependency ordering only; no real external infrastructure, no AI output dependency, no multi-step agentic flow.
behaviors.test_description: Set up plugins A(depends on B) and B in a temp directory. Load both via framework and record `onLoad` invocation sequence. Assert sequence has B before A.

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: dependency-graph cycle handling and initialization gating error across resolver and loader executor. Required setup: two plugin manifests with mutual dependency and observable load markers. Unit is too low because the required failure includes both cycle report and suppression of loading. Higher levels rejected: behavior is internal graph/orchestration logic with local files only; no subprocess/network/OS-specific behavior, no AI dependency, no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A->B and B->A with entry/onLoad side-effect markers. Run loader. Assert cycle error is reported and neither plugin import nor `onLoad` marker is observed.

behaviors.description: A plugin's `onLoad` hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: lifecycle sequencing error between module import/execute phase and hook invocation phase. Required setup: plugin entry module with import-time side effect and `onLoad` hook asserting that side effect is already present. Unit is too low because this is specifically a boundary between runtime import execution and lifecycle dispatcher. Higher levels considered/rejected: no need for subprocess or external OS/network behavior; the behavior text is in-process plugin lifecycle ordering only. No AI dependency or agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Build a plugin whose entry module sets a global/file marker during import; `onLoad` checks marker state and records timestamp/order. Load plugin and assert marker was set before `onLoad` runs.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its `onLoad` hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: error-isolation and dependency-aware continuation orchestration error. Required setup: one failing plugin, one independent plugin, and one dependent plugin; verify continuation for independent plugin and skip/block for dependent plugin according to dependency graph. Unit is too low because behavior requires interaction of hook execution, error propagation policy, and dependency filtering. Higher levels rejected: no real network/subprocess/OS-only condition, no AI/ML output quality dependence, no multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins: F throws in `onLoad`; I has no dependency on F; D depends on F. Run loader. Assert F failure is reported, I still initializes, and D is not initialized due to dependency on failed F.
